---
title: "Installing hvtiPlotR"
author: "John Ehrlinger <ehrlinj@ccf.org>"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    html-math-method: mathjax
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 80
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Installing hvtiPlotR}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE)
```

This vignette walks through installing **hvtiPlotR** — from a minimum
working install for end-users, through a full install with all suggested
packages, to a developer checkout. It closes with verification steps and
troubleshooting for the most common issues.

**hvtiPlotR** is an internal Cleveland Clinic package and is not on CRAN.
All installs go through GitHub.

## Requirements

- **R** ≥ 4.1.0
- **Pandoc** (bundled with RStudio; otherwise install separately)
- **quarto** CLI — required only to rebuild vignettes from source; not
  needed for normal end-user installs
- Write access to your R library. Check with `.libPaths()`.

Imports are installed automatically from CRAN: `ComplexUpset`,
`ggalluvial`, `ggplot2` (≥ 3.4.0), `grid`, `rlang` (≥ 1.0.0), `survival`,
`officer` (≥ 0.6.0), `rvg` (≥ 0.2.5).

## Standard install

Install from GitHub using the
[remotes](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=remotes) package:

```r
install.packages("remotes")   # if not already installed
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR")
```

This installs all `Imports`. Suggested packages (vignette builder,
optional plot dependencies) are not installed by default — see the next
section if you want the full feature set.

## Install with suggested packages

To install everything the vignettes and optional plot families rely on,
request suggested dependencies. One of the suggests (`ggsankey`) is only
on GitHub, so enable the `Remotes` field during install:

```r
remotes::install_github(
  "ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR",
  dependencies = TRUE                # install Suggests too
)
```

`dependencies = TRUE` pulls:

- `knitr`, `quarto` — vignette building
- `testthat` (≥ 3.0.0), `devtools`, `pkgload`, `here` — test + dev tooling
- `ggsankey` — cluster-stability Sankey (`hv_sankey()`); comes from
  `davidsjoberg/ggsankey` via the `Remotes` field
- `haven`, `patchwork`, `scales`, `tidyr` — data import and plot
  composition

If the `ggsankey` resolution fails, install it directly first:

```r
remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey")
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR", dependencies = TRUE)
```

## Developer install

For contributors working on the package itself:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR.git
cd hvtiPlotR
```

Then from R, inside the package directory:

```r
install.packages(c("devtools", "remotes"))
remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey")  # GitHub-only Suggest
devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)       # CRAN Imports + Suggests
devtools::load_all()
devtools::check()
```

For the full contributor workflow (branching, review, CI) see
`vignette("contributing", package = "hvtiPlotR")`.

## Verifying the install

After installing, run the following to confirm the package loads and a
theme applies cleanly:

```r
library(hvtiPlotR)

# Package version should match what was just installed
packageVersion("hvtiPlotR")

# Apply a theme to a ggplot
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  hv_theme("manuscript")
```

If the PowerPoint export path is part of your workflow, smoke-test it
against the bundled template:

```r
p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() + hv_theme("ppt")

save_ppt(
  object     = p,
  template   = system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR"),
  powerpoint = tempfile(fileext = ".pptx")
)
```

List installed vignettes:

```r
vignette(package = "hvtiPlotR")
```

You should see at least: `installation`, `hvtiPlotR`, `plot-functions`,
`plot-decorators`, `contributing`.

## Updating

Re-run the same install command. `remotes::install_github()` always
fetches the latest commit on the default branch:

```r
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR")
```

To install a specific branch, tag, or commit:

```r
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR@v2.0.1")
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR@engineering")
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR@<commit-sha>")
```

To uninstall:

```r
remove.packages("hvtiPlotR")
```

## Troubleshooting

**`remotes::install_github()` fails with a 403 / rate-limit error.**
GitHub rate-limits anonymous API calls. Set a personal access token:

```r
Sys.setenv(GITHUB_PAT = "ghp_...")
remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR")
```

**Behind a corporate proxy.** Set `http_proxy` / `https_proxy` before
launching R, or configure `options(download.file.method = "libcurl")`.

**`quarto` not found when building vignettes.** Install the quarto CLI
from <https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/>. Package-installed
`quarto` (the R wrapper) calls out to the CLI binary.

**`ggsankey` does not install.** It is GitHub-only. Install it first:

```r
remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey")
```

**`officer` / `rvg` install fails (system libxml/libcurl).** On
Debian/Ubuntu, install system headers:

```bash
sudo apt-get install libxml2-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev
```

On macOS, Xcode command-line tools provide these:

```bash
xcode-select --install
```

**Permission denied writing to the library path.** Either run R with
appropriate permissions or configure a user library via `.libPaths()` /
`R_LIBS_USER`.

## Reporting installation problems

Open an issue at <https://github.com/ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR/issues> and
include the output of:

```r
sessionInfo()
```
